questions.question: What is the definition of a line segment?
questions.answer: A line segment is a part of a line that has a fixed length and ends at two distinct points. It is denoted by the symbol 'AB' where A and B are the endpoints.

questions.question: How many types of angles are there?
questions.answer: There are three types of angles: acute, right, and obtuse. Acute angles have measures less than 90 degrees, right angles measure exactly 90 degrees, and obtuse angles have measures greater than 90 degrees.

questions.question: What is the difference between a chord and a diameter?
questions.answer: A chord is a line segment that connects two points on a circle, while a diameter is a line segment that passes through the center of a circle and has its endpoints on the circumference.

questions.question: What is the law of large numbers?
questions.answer: The law of large numbers states that as the number of trials increases, outcomes get closer to the expected average. This means that if you flip a fair coin many times, the results will likely be very close to 50% heads.

questions.question: What is an accelerator?
questions.answer: An accelerometer is a device that measures acceleration, which is the rate of change of velocity. It is used to measure the movement of objects and can be found in many applications such as smartphones and cars.

questions.question: How do you measure the distance between two points?
questions.answer: To measure the distance between two points, you can use a ruler or a measuring tape. The distance is the length of the line segment that connects the two points.

questions.question: What are some real-world applications of geometry?
questions.answer: Geometry is used in many real-world applications such as architecture, engineering, and computer graphics. It helps us design buildings, bridges, and other structures that are safe and functional.

questions.question: What is the difference between a ray and a line?
questions.answer: A ray is a line that extends infinitely in one direction, while a line is a set of points that extend infinitely in two directions.

questions.question: What are some properties of parallel lines?
questions.answer: Parallel lines never intersect and have the same slope. They are always equidistant apart.

questions.question: How do you identify congruent triangles?
questions.answer: Congruent triangles have the same size and shape. They can be identified by comparing their corresponding sides and angles.

questions.question: What are some properties of a circle?
questions.answer: A circle is a set of points that are equidistant from the center. It has no beginning or end and its circumference is always curved.

questions.question: How do you measure the area of a shape?
questions.answer: To measure the area of a shape, you can use various formulas such as the formula for the area of a rectangle (length x width) or the formula for the area of a circle (πr^2).

questions.question: What is the difference between a tangent and a secant?
questions.answer: A tangent is a line that touches a circle at exactly one point, while a secant is a line that intersects a circle at two points.

questions.question: How do you calculate the volume of a shape?
questions.answer: To calculate the volume of a shape, you can use various formulas such as the formula for the volume of a cube (side^3) or the formula for the volume of a sphere (4/3πr^3).

questions.question: What is the law of sines?
questions.answer: The law of sines states that in a triangle, the ratio of the length of a side to the sine of its opposite angle is constant for all three sides and angles. This means that if you know two sides and their opposite angles, you can calculate the third side.
